evals.prompt: Run regression QA on the project at /tmp/eval-fastapi-app. It's a FastAPI app I inherited and I have no idea what's tested. Single pass only.
evals.expected_output: Spec files written to regression-qa/specs/, test files written to regression-qa/tests/, run_tests output showing pass/fail counts. Summary at the end.

evals.prompt: QA this CLI tool at /tmp/eval-click-cli — use ralph loop, run until green or 3 iterations max
evals.expected_output: Spec file for CLI commands, tests using CliRunner or subprocess, healer invoked on any failures, final green summary or real-bug annotations.

evals.prompt: I have a Flask REST API at /tmp/eval-flask-api. Write regression tests for all the endpoints. Don't loop, just generate them and tell me what you found.
evals.expected_output: Planner produces spec with all routes, Generator writes pytest tests using Flask test client, test results reported. No healer loop.
